displayName: NBD Pro — user-facing function latency p95 high

documentation.content:
p95 request latency on a user-facing callable is elevated — users are seeing spinners/timeouts. The functions-error-rate policy only catches THROWN errors, not slow/timed-out calls, so this is the complementary signal.

Common causes:
  - renderPdf: Chromium cold start or a large multi-page/photo doc near the 60s timeout.
  - claudeProxy / analyzePhotoVision: Anthropic API latency or a large prompt/image.
  - cold starts after a deploy (consider minInstances on the hot paths).
  - an upstream (Stripe/Twilio/Groq) slow-down.

Investigate:
  1. Cloud Monitoring → Metrics Explorer → run.googleapis.com/request_latencies, group by service_name, to see which function.
  2. Cloud Logging for that service — correlate slow requests to payload size / upstream calls.
  3. If it started right after a deploy, consider rolling back (see the rollback runbook).

Threshold is intentionally below each function's hard timeout so you hear about it BEFORE requests start 504-ing.
